---
title: "sqlite_postgres_migration"
author: "Jessica Mahone"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

## Prerequisites
This migration moves data from SQLite to PostgreSQL. Before running, execute 
`postgres_migration_cleaning.Rmd` to audit, clean, and export the source data 
to `data/processed/`. This migration script then loads those cleaned files 
and writes them to Postgres.

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

library(DBI)
library(RPostgres)
library(tidyverse)

# Connect to Postgres
pg_con <- dbConnect(RPostgres::Postgres(),
                    dbname = "local_news_100_communities",
                    host = "localhost",
                    port = 5432,
                    user = "jessicamahone",
                    password = "")

```

```{r}
# Read cleaned tables
communities_clean <- read_csv("data/processed/communities_clean.csv")
outlets_clean <- read_csv("data/processed/outlets_clean.csv")
stories_clean <- read_csv("data/processed/stories_clean.csv")

```

```{sql connection=pg_con}

CREATE TABLE communities (
  community_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  city TEXT NOT NULL,
  state TEXT NOT NULL,
  population INTEGER NOT NULL,
  area_sqm NUMERIC,
  density_pop_per_sqm NUMERIC,
  closest_large_media_market TEXT,
  distance_from_lmm INTEGER,
  pct_white NUMERIC,
  pct_black NUMERIC,
  pct_hislat NUMERIC,
  universities_count INTEGER,
  county_seat BOOLEAN,
  state_capital BOOLEAN
);

```

```{sql connection=pg_con}

CREATE TABLE outlets (
  outlet_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  outlet_name TEXT,
  url TEXT,
  outlet_type TEXT CHECK (outlet_type IN ('Radio Station', 'Newspaper/Magazine', 'Online Only', 'TV Station')),
  community_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES communities(community_id)
);

```

```{sql connection=pg_con}

CREATE TABLE stories (
  story_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  publication_date DATE,
  headline TEXT,
  outlet_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES outlets(outlet_id),
  original BOOLEAN,
  local BOOLEAN,
  critical_info_need TEXT CHECK (critical_info_need IN ('None', 'Emergencies/risks', 'Political life', 'Civic information', 'Economic development', 'Health', 'Education', 'Environment/planning', 'Transportation systems'))
);

```


```{r}

# Write tables to Postgres.

dbWriteTable(pg_con, "communities", communities_clean, append = TRUE, row.names = FALSE)
dbWriteTable(pg_con, "outlets", outlets_clean, append = TRUE, row.names = FALSE)
dbWriteTable(pg_con, "stories", stories_clean, append = TRUE, row.names = FALSE)

```

```{r}
dbGetQuery(pg_con, "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM communities")
dbGetQuery(pg_con, "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM outlets")
dbGetQuery(pg_con, "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM stories")
```

```{sql connection=pg_con}

-- Check foreign keys are working
SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM stories s
JOIN outlets o ON s.outlet_id = o.outlet_id
JOIN communities c ON o.community_id = c.community_id;

```

```{sql connection=pg_con}

-- Check outlet_type constraint is enforced
SELECT DISTINCT outlet_type FROM outlets;

```

```{sql connection=pg_con}

-- Check boolean conversion worked
SELECT original, local, COUNT(*) 
FROM stories 
GROUP BY original, local;

```
